text: Shakespeare wrote plays and sonnets in early modern English.
Hamlet explores grief doubt and the search for truth.
Macbeth shows how ambition can turn into destruction.
King Lear focuses on family loyalty pride and loss.
Othello studies jealousy trust and manipulation.
Romeo and Juliet presents love conflict and tragedy.
The Tempest combines magic forgiveness and power.
Much Ado About Nothing uses wit misunderstanding and romance.
Shakespeare characters often speak in memorable metaphors.
His works remain widely performed studied and adapted today.
